steps: Launch the app via scripts/launch-toaster-monitored.ps1.
Open Settings and confirm an 'Export' entry appears in the sidebar.
Click Export and confirm the ExportSettings panel renders with at least the Loudness target Select.
Launch the app and open eval/fixtures/toaster_example.mp4 (60 s fixture).
Open the export dialog and click 'Run preflight'.
Within 5 s, confirm three numeric readouts appear: integrated LUFS, true-peak dBTP, and LRA.
Open a fixture known to be loud (e.g. integrated LUFS near -6).
Set Loudness target to Streaming (-14 LUFS).
Run preflight; confirm a non-blocking warning appears reporting >12 LU off target.
Confirm the Export button is still enabled.
Run: rg 'loudnorm=' src
Confirm zero matches (no TS code constructs the loudnorm filter string).
Open eval/fixtures/toaster_example.mp4.
Set Loudness target to Streaming (-14 LUFS) in Export Settings.
Export to a temp mp4.
Re-open the exported file and run preflight; confirm integrated LUFS reads within 1.0 LU of -14.
Place a settings.json containing 'normalize_audio_on_export: true' (no loudness_target field) in the app data dir.
Launch the app and open Settings -> Export.
Confirm the Loudness target Select shows 'Podcast (-16 LUFS)' as the active value.
Run: rg -n 'ebur128|integrated_lufs|truePeak|true_peak|LRA|lra' src
Confirm matches are limited to type declarations and display formatters (toFixed/Intl.NumberFormat).
Confirm no arithmetic operator (+, -, *, /) is applied to these fields outside a formatter.
